tokens: (Reading Surface 11-1) "Verify EVA circuit breaker configuration." If you can move forward, I can get in there and verify mine.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Are you trying to get your O2 off?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, my circuit breakers are okay except Fan 2 to Suit Fan Delta-P, which we'll pick up (in just a few seconds).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay; "doff the gloves and stow on the comm panel."
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'm going to have to use the purge valve.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let me get out of your way.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It (meaning the suit pressure)'s coming down, but very slowly.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Okay; put you in high (purge valve) flow, here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; let's take off your gloves.
(Long Pause) I'll "verify the safety" (on the forward
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Okay, Descent Water is coming open.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay; and the next thing is "connect the LM O2 hoses; red to red and blue to blue".
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Red to red and blue to blue.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, we've got to do a dump.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We better make sure we got them (the LM hoses) under the OPS (hose)...(correcting himself) PLSS (hose).
Okay; I go to Suit Flow.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Doesn't look like my prime water is off.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Doesn't feel like the prime water is off.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I've got bare hands, now, when I get back here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It was off, and you must have bumped it, maybe.
(Pause) It was off, but you are getting water, all right.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I feel it running down my leg.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I mean it wasn't off then, but I remember turning it off, and it went off.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, it's probably catching up on something, Dave, just like it did yesterday...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: My O2 hose is disconnected, and going to Suit Flow.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: "Disconnect PLSS water from PGA and connect LM water to PGA."
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: How about a little (LM) LCG pump action?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Your (LM oxygen) flow ought to cool you there; it's cooling me pretty good.

tokens: Audio circuit-breaker, open, and connect to LM comm."
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, Audio panels for both of us, "VHF A to Receive and B to..."
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; could we have the Power Amp, On, please.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We observe the batteries coming on; and I have a lift-off time when you're ready.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I'm ready to copy the LM ascent PAD (as per Surface 12-4).
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay; I don't have an ascent PAD for you, but I have the lift-off time.

tokens: And I have some star changes for the P57.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: For the P57 coming up right now, let's use
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And, Falcon, when you get around to it, how about an ED batt, check, please (as per Surface 12-1)?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And, Ed, an E(rasable)-memory dump coming down (as per Surface 12-1).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We're ready to give you an uplink (as per Surface 12-2) if we can have P00 (Program Zero-Zero) and Data.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Looks like we need to do a Verb 96.
You're going through an integration loop.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I thought that P00 and Data might get you in trouble.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We're going to delete the VHF check at this pass just to give you a little more time.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We just won't bother the LM with it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Do you have the torquing angles?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: How are you coming with your stowage (as per Surface 12-3)?
We have these locations available still, if you're not too well stowed up yet.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Well, we were just mushing along with it by the checklist here, and why don't you go through what you have in mind?
We'll take a listen to what you got here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: What we were expecting was bags 4 and 6 in the
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Ed, let me tell you where we have them.

tokens: The aft engine cover is bag 3.
(Pause) And the one remaining bag, we figured we'd put in the ISA.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That would either be bag 4 or 6, and we seem to have both of them indicated for the ISA.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, in other words, you'd like bags 4 and 6 in the ISA.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: That's great, and it sounds like the rest of it's as per plan.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And be advised we're showing total weight of slightly in excess of 230 pounds, which is a little over what we expected.
However, by deleting a plane change, we're going to have plenty of fuel for it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Give us a little bit of idea of where you are, Jim.
We're starting to worry about time a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We're just configuring circuit breakers, right now.

tokens: Have you got a weight that we can have?
